task: Strict visual review of the full-face scaffold extracted from user annotated geometry references.
visual_review_findings: The extracted scaffold overlay matches the user-provided red visible-face contour, yellow shared eye band, pink brow/forehead separator, green nose guides, and cyan mouth/lip guide positions on the clean source image.
The red face contour now provides the missing global reference frame that previous isolated eye and eyelid traces did not have.
The yellow shared eye band correctly records the user's intended bilateral eye-plane scaffold, but it is still a guide band, not an accepted eye mask.

The semantic extraction panel separates nose, lips, eyes, brows, neck, and face/skin evidence enough for reference use, but these translucent semantic regions are not final masks and must not be promoted.
No active ComfyUI/input mask was changed. No generated-output proof was run. No row received W70_MASK_PROMOTION_ROW_GATE_PASS_TRUE.
required_next_step: Derive one future candidate mask from this full-face scaffold, then render a row-specific source/mask/overlay/protected-boundary panel before any generated-output or promotion work.
blocked_uses: Do not treat the scaffold as a final segmentation mask.
Do not use the extracted semantic fills as active ComfyUI masks.
Do not mark eye, brow, nose, mouth, face, jaw, or neck rows pass-like from this evidence alone.
